adapter is not the Keycloak server product.

commit.message:
fix(test): use deterministic UUID in setupStandardMocks to prevent CPR pattern flakiness

UUID.randomUUID() could generate a UUID where a 6-digit decimal run in segment 1
spans the hyphen into a 4-digit segment 2, matching the CPR pattern \d{6}-\d{4}.
For example: 0b821041-2405... contains '821041-2405'.

Fix: replace with a fixed all-hex-letter UUID (cccccccc-cccc-4ccc-8ccc-cccccccccccc)
that structurally cannot match any CPR or 10-digit pattern.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): use fully-qualified Maven coordinates for OWASP plugin

Maven cannot resolve 'dependency-check:' prefix because org.owasp is not
in the default plugin groups (org.apache.maven.plugins, org.codehaus.mojo).
Use full groupId:artifactId:goal syntax to invoke the plugin directly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(catala): disambiguate exception hierarchy with label a2_base

In scope AfbrydelseValidering, afbrydelseResultat had two unlabeled base
definitions (line 299: BEROSTILLELSE+PSRM, line 331: LOENINDEHOLDELSE_VARSEL),
causing 'Ambiguous exception' errors for all 4 exceptions.

Fix: add 'label a2_base' to the line-331 base definition (A-2), then
change all 4 exception blocks to 'exception a2_base' explicitly.

Exceptions A-2b (LOENINDEHOLDELSE_AFGOERELSE+!registered),
A-3 (LOENINDEHOLDELSE_AFGOERELSE+registered),
A-4 (LOENINDEHOLDELSE_AFGOERELSE+harInaktivSiden),
A-5a (UDLAEG+ORDINARY) now unambiguously override A-2.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): fix duration syntax — use 'N day' not 'duration of N days'

In Catala 1.1.0, duration literals use singular unit keywords:
  365 day (not 365 days, not duration of 365 days)
  3 * 365 day = integer * duration = 1095-day duration

'duration of' is not a Catala construct — it was treated as a variable
reference 'duration' followed by the 'of' operator, causing parse errors.

Changes in ga_2_4_foraeldelse.catala_da:
- Remove 'duration of ()' construct; keep inner arithmetic (9 occurrences)
- Change 'days' -> 'day' throughout
- Add missing 'consequence' before standalone 'equals' at line 605

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): remove (* *) inline comments invalid in Catala 1.1.0 parser

Remove 65 inline (* ... *) comments from scope context declarations in
ga_2_3_2_1_daekningsraekkefoeigen.catala_da and ga_2_4_foraeldelse.catala_da.

Catala 1.1.0 parser does not accept (* *) at end of context declarations;
valid tokens after a type name are 'input', 'output', 'internal', 'state', etc.
The comments were explanatory only and do not affect logic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(catala): correct Catala 1.1.0 definition/rule syntax

- Conditional non-boolean: rule → definition, keep 'consequence equals V'
  (definition X under condition C consequence equals V)
- Boolean: rule stays, consequence equals true/false →
  consequence fulfilled/not fulfilled
- Unconditional non-boolean (no under condition): collapse to single-line
  'definition X equals V' without consequence keyword

commit.message:
fix: don't bump chat to top of sidebar on open

MarkChatChanged no longer touches UpdatedAt — it only marks the chat
dirty for persistence. The timestamp bump now lives in QueueSaveChat
and SaveCurrentChatAsync where touchIndex: true already identifies
real user/assistant activity.

Metadata-only saves (model selection, plan content, session creation,
title generation, agent/skill/project changes) go through the same
MarkChatChanged but never set touchIndex, so UpdatedAt stays put and
the sidebar order is preserved.

commit.message:
Fix ghost chat accumulation in sidebar

Clean up orphaned chats on startup — chats whose message file is
missing or empty on disk are removed from the index. These ghost
entries accumulated when the index was saved but the per-chat
message file was never written (e.g. due to a save race or the
process being killed between the two writes).

Additionally, guard the independent index save in title generation
so it only persists when the chat's message file already exists,
preventing the race condition that created most ghost entries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix viewport rebuild aborting update() on empty tile cells

staticTileGrid cells can be undefined for valid map positions that
simply have no tiles. The guard was using 'return' which exited the
entire update() function, leaving cachedStaticEntities empty and
cachedViewportKey un-updated. Every subsequent frame at the same
player position hit the same early exit, making characters/NPCs/items
disappear until a full refresh reset the key. Changed to 'continue'
in both the old render() and new update() viewport-rebuild loops.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix canvas size, tile positioning, and remove pre-render ground pass

- initPixi(): use viewportController dimensions instead of hardcoded
  800x600 defaults; call resizeCanvases() after DOM insertion so the
  canvas CSS size is correct from the first frame (fixes tiny canvas
  on main menu)

- tileScreenPosition(): add missing -HALF_TILE_WIDTH / -HALF_TILE_HEIGHT
  and tile.xOffset / tile.yOffset to match the original renderTile()
  formula (fixes misaligned ground, shadow, and object layers)

- update(): remove pre-render ground+shadow path entirely; ground and
  shadow tiles now flow through addTileSprite() like every other layer,
  eliminating the renderGround() canvas compositing step and
  associated groundTextures/groundDirty bookkeeping in the render loop

commit.message:
Fix mirrored sprite positioning for characters and NPCs

The drawX formula for the mirrored case was carried over from the
Canvas 2D ctx.translate(gameWidth,0)/ctx.scale(-1,1) trick, which
is incompatible with PixiJS's scale.x = -1.

With PixiJS default anchor (0,0) and scale.x = -1, the sprite draws
leftward from sprite.x, occupying [sprite.x - w, sprite.x]. To place
the left edge at screenX + mirroredXOffset the correct formula is:

  sprite.x = screenX + frame.mirroredXOffset + frame.w

The old formula (gameWidth - screenX - mirroredXOffset) caused mirrored
entities to move in the opposite direction as the viewport scrolled,
producing the 'sliding' effect reported for Right/Up-facing NPCs and
characters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate rendering pipeline from Canvas 2D to PixiJS

- Bootstrap PixiJS Application in client.ts with worldContainer,
  uiContainer, and minimapContainer scene hierarchy
- Replace requestAnimationFrame loop with app.ticker fixed-timestep
  accumulator in main.ts
- Atlas: replace HTMLImageElement with CanvasSource + Texture;
  add getAtlasTexture(), getFrameTexture(), getSubTexture() caching
- MapRenderer: add SpritePool and update() method with sprite-based
  rendering for all entity types (tiles, characters, NPCs, items,
  cursor, effects, health bars, emotes, nameplates, player menu)
- renderGround(): produce CanvasSource/Texture in parallel with
  existing HTMLImageElement path; ground now blitted as Sprite
- MinimapRenderer: add update() method using sprite pool for all
  minimap icons with correct alpha handling
- Font.getSprite(): render text to offscreen canvas and return Sprite
- ChatBubble.getSprite(): cache CanvasSource/Texture, return Sprite
- viewport-controller: resize via app.renderer.resize() instead of
  canvas width/height mutation
- mouse-controller: use app.renderer.canvas for hit-test coordinates

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fix factually incorrect NOTE: workload apply/push/reconcile do NOT
  expand ${VAR} from local env vars. They pass manifests through;
  Flux performs substitution in-cluster via postBuild.
- Fix frontmatter casing: use backticks for `workload validate` and
  capitalize KSail to match existing guide conventions.
- Clarify schema inference: typed placeholders require cached schemas.
  Core and CRD resources fall back to string "placeholder" when their
  schemas have not yet been cached.
